item.title: Morning Briefing 2026-03-12
item.description:
Good morning. It is Tuesday, March 11th, 2026. Welcome to your morning market briefing.
We begin with a major development in global energy markets. The International Energy Agency has proposed the largest release of oil from strategic reserves in its history. This action aims to bring down crude prices that have risen during the ongoing conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran. Oil prices have experienced significant volatility in recent sessions. A deleted tweet from U.S. Energy Se

item.title: Morning Briefing 2026-03-12
item.description:
Good morning. Today is Tuesday, March 11th, 2026. Welcome to your morning market briefing.
We begin with the major story driving global energy markets this morning. The International Energy Agency has proposed the largest release of oil reserves in its history. This coordinated action aims to bring down crude prices that have surged during the ongoing conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran. The proposed release exceeds any previous IEA intervention, including those during the Gulf

item.title: Morning Briefing 2026-03-11
item.description:
Good morning. It is Tuesday, March 10th, 2026. Welcome to your morning financial briefing.
We begin today with developments in energy markets and the ongoing conflict in the Middle East, which continues to dominate market attention.
President Trump stated last night that the war with Iran will end "very soon," though he indicated the U.S. military campaign still has further to go. These comments came amid significant volatility in oil markets. Oil prices surged during the session before pullin

item.title: Morning Briefing 2026-03-10
item.description:
Good morning. It is Monday, March 9th, 2026. Welcome to your morning market briefing.
We begin with the dominant story affecting global markets: the ongoing military conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran, now entering its second week.
Oil prices have climbed above one hundred dollars per barrel following disruptions to shipping traffic through the Strait of Hormuz. This represents what analysts are describing as the most severe shock to energy markets since the 1970s. The disrup

item.title: Morning Briefing 2026-03-09
item.description:
Good morning. Today is Monday, March 10th, 2026. Welcome to your morning financial briefing.
We begin with the developing situation in the Middle East, where the ongoing conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran continues to dominate headlines and move markets.
The U.S.-Israeli air campaign against Iran is now in its second week, with Israeli strikes targeting fuel storage facilities in Tehran. Iranian authorities have responded by cutting motorists' fuel allowances at the pump and

item.title: Morning Briefing 2026-03-09
item.description:
Good morning. Today is Monday, March 10th, 2026. Welcome to your morning market briefing.
We begin with the dominant story affecting global markets: the ongoing military conflict in the Persian Gulf. US and Israeli forces continue conducting airstrikes on Iranian targets, with Tehran's historic capital sustaining significant damage from thousands of munitions. Israeli strikes on fuel storage facilities have prompted Iranian authorities to reduce fuel allowances for motorists and issue warnings

item.title: Morning Briefing 2026-03-08
item.description:
Good morning. Today is Friday, March 7th, 2026. Welcome to your morning market briefing.
We begin with the dominant story affecting global markets: the ongoing conflict in the Persian Gulf and its impact on energy prices and broader market conditions.
**MIDDLE EAST CONFLICT AND ENERGY MARKETS**
Oil prices this week rose at their fastest pace on record as the war between the United States and Iran continues to disrupt supply from the Persian Gulf region. Approximately one-fifth of the world's

item.title: Morning Briefing 2026-03-08
item.description:
Good morning. It is Saturday, March 7th, 2026. Welcome to your morning briefing.
We begin this morning with extensive coverage of the ongoing conflict in the Middle East and its widening economic impact.
**GEOPOLITICS: THE IRAN CONFLICT**
The conflict with Iran continues to escalate across multiple fronts this morning. According to reporting from The Wall Street Journal, Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and his military commanders have activated what is being described as a high-risk, no-l

item.title: Morning Briefing 2026-03-08
item.description: Test update to refresh RSS feed

item.title: Morning Briefing 2026-03-08

item.title: Morning Briefing 2026-03-08
item.description:
Good morning. It is Saturday, March 7th, 2026. Welcome to your morning briefing.
We begin with developments in the Middle East, where the conflict between the United States and Iran continues to shape global markets and geopolitical calculations.
Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and military commanders have activated what analysts describe as a no-limits defense strategy. This plan, developed prior to the current conflict, includes a decentralized military structure that allows Iranian forc

item.title: Morning Briefing 2026-03-08
item.description:
Good morning. Today is Friday, March 7th, 2026. Welcome to your morning briefing.
We begin with the conflict in the Middle East, which continues to dominate headlines and is now reshaping global markets.
Iran's Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and military commanders have activated what reports describe as a high-risk strategy designed to allow Iranian forces to continue operations even if top commanders are eliminated. This plan, characterized as a decentralized "mosaic defense," is now in effect.

item.title: Morning Briefing 2026-03-08
item.description:
Good morning. Today is Friday, March 7th, 2026. Welcome to your morning briefing.
We begin with the conflict in the Middle East, which continues to dominate global headlines and markets.
Iran has activated what reports describe as a high-risk military strategy designed to continue operations even if top commanders are eliminated. According to reporting from the Wall Street Journal, Iranian Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei and his military commanders implemented what is being called a decentralized
